response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a capable, long-tenured polyglot engineer with entrepreneurial streaks and recent exposure to AI-agent tooling, but the role demands a director-level AI strategist with deep, recognized expertise in AI/ML, cloud platform leadership, and executive-facing advisory experience. His AI involvement is nascent (MCP tooling at re:cinq since April 2025) rather than foundational, and his career arc has been that of a hands-on individual contributor across many stacks — not a thought leader shaping AI product strategy at scale. There is no evidence of advising strategic customers, driving organizational AI vision, or publishing/presenting recognized AI expertise, all of which are central to this role.
strengths: ~17 years of professional software engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure, satisfying the minimum tenure requirement
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling at re:cinq signals genuine engagement with the agentic AI space
Demonstrated entrepreneurial instinct via founding GISCollective and cross-domain product delivery in resource-constrained startup environments
concerns: AI expertise is superficial and very recent — limited to developer tooling in a single role started one month ago, with no history of building or shipping AI-powered products at scale

No evidence of director-level or strategic scope: no advisory relationships with enterprise customers, no engineering org leadership, and no track record of influencing product or platform strategy beyond the team level
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and no markers of recognized external expertise (publications, keynotes, flagship AI products) that the preferred qualifications explicitly call out
